titleStmt.title: Taishō Tripiṭaka
大正新脩大藏經
信心銘
Taishō Tripiṭaka, Electronic version, No. 2010 信心銘
大正新脩大藏經數位版, No. 2010 信心銘
titleStmt.author: 隋 僧璨作

distributor.name: 財團法人佛教電子佛典基金會 (CBETA)

availability.p: Available for non-commercial use when distributed with this header intact.

projectDesc.p: Text as provided by Mr. Hsiao Chen-Kuo, Text as provided by Electronic Buddhadharma Society (EBS), Text as provided by Anonymous from USA
蕭鎮國大德提供，佛教電腦資訊庫功德會提供，北美某大德提供

revisionDesc.change: CWRay Chou 周邦信P4 to P5 conversion by p4top5a.py, intended for publication
CW (ed.) Created initial TEI XML version with BASICX.BAT (00/01/24)

lg.l: The supreme Way is not difficult, it only avoids picking and choosing; just do not hate or love
Then it is clearly understood; the slightest deviation is as far apart as heaven and earth
If you wish it to appear before you, do not hold to conformity or opposition, for conflict arises between the two
This is the sickness of mind; not knowing the profound meaning, you vainly labor to quiet thoughts
Perfect like the vast void, lacking nothing and nothing in excess, it is only due to grasping and rejecting
Therefore, it is better not to follow conditions, nor dwell in empty endurance
With a single even mind, distinctions vanish by themselves; stopping movement returns to stillness
But stopping makes movement even more active; stuck in the two extremes, how could one know the One
If the One is not understood, both sides lose their function; rejecting being or non-being
Clinging to emptiness and turning away from it, with much talk and much thought, one becomes ever less in accord
Cut off words and thoughts, and nothing is not penetrated; returning to the root, you grasp the essence
Following illumination, you lose the source; turning the light back in an instant surpasses the former emptiness
The prior emptiness transforms due to deluded views; there is no need to seek truth
You need only cease views; do not abide in dual views, and be careful not to pursue them
As soon as right and wrong arise, the mind is lost in confusion; duality comes from the One
Yet do not cling even to the One; when the one mind does not arise, all dharmas are without fault
Without fault and without dharmas, not arising and not mind, it can vanish with conditions
Objects follow the subject and sink; objects arise from the subject, the subject arises from objects
If you wish to know these two, originally they are one emptiness; this one emptiness contains both
It equally contains all forms, seeing no fine or coarse—how could there be partiality
The great Way is vast and broad, neither easy nor difficult; small views are full of doubt
The more haste, the more delay; clinging to it loses measure, and the mind enters a wrong path
Let it go and it is naturally so; its essence neither comes nor goes; following nature accords with the Way
Free and at ease, free from trouble; clinging thoughts stray from truth; dull sinking is not good
Since it is not good, why weary the spirit; why distinguish close and distant if you wish to approach the One Vehicle
Do not hate the six sense-objects; if the six sense-objects are not hated, they are the same as true awakening
The wise do not act; the foolish bind themselves; the dharma is not different from dharma
Deludedly clinging and attaching, using mind upon mind—is this not a great error
Delusion gives rise to stillness and disturbance; awakening has no liking or disliking; all dualities
Are vainly judged; like dreams, illusions, and empty flowers—why bother to grasp them
Gain and loss, right and wrong—let them all go at once; if the eyes do not sleep
All dreams vanish by themselves; if the mind is not divided, all dharmas are one thusness
The essence of suchness is mysterious; suddenly forgetting conditions, all dharmas are seen equally
Returning to what is natural, its reasons disappear; it cannot be compared or measured
Stopping movement is not movement; movement in stopping is not stopping; when both do not stand
How could there be a One; at the ultimate end, no rules remain
When the mind of equality is opened, all actions cease; doubt is completely purified
True faith is upright and balanced; nothing is retained, nothing can be remembered
Empty and luminous, naturally so; no need for mental effort; not a place of thought
Consciousness and feeling are hard to fathom; in the dharma realm of suchness there is no other and no self
To quickly accord, it is only said to be non-dual; in non-duality all is the same
Nothing is not included; wise ones in all directions enter this teaching
This teaching is neither hurried nor delayed; it is neither present nor absent一念萬年萬年一念
Before your eyes in all directions, the extremely small is the same as the great; delusion cuts off distinctions
The extremely great is the same as the small, boundaries are not seen; being is precisely non-being
Non-being is precisely being; if it is not so, there is no need to maintain it
One is all, all is one; if you can be thus
Why worry it will not be complete; faith in mind is not dual, non-duality is faith in mind
The path of words is cut off; it is not past, present, or future

cb:juan.cb:jhead: End of the Inscription on Faith in Mind

p.note: 【原】慶安元年刊宗教大學藏本，【甲】享保十九年刊駒澤大學藏本
